posterior tibial tendon dysfunction runners return to running pubmed
hallux rigidus athlete management review pubmed
coverage_notes: Expanded coverage: original 6 papers covered plantar fasciitis exclusively (treatment meta, risk factors meta, high-load RCT, stepped-care guideline, 2 biomechanics studies). Added 6 papers covering other foot conditions: running injury epidemiology SR (Kakouris 2021), Morton's neuroma non-surgical treatment SR+MA (Matthews 2019), navicular stress fracture RTS SR+MA (Attia 2021), foot orthoses injury prevention SR+MA (Bonanno 2017), PTTD return to running review (Espinosa 2015), hallux rigidus in athletes (Murawski 2024). Now covers PF + Morton's neuroma + navicular fractures + PTTD + hallux rigidus + orthoses + general epidemiology.

papers.title: Effects of therapeutic interventions on pain due to plantar fasciitis: A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Largest meta-analysis of PF treatment (236 RCTs, 15401 patients); found ESWT was the only intervention effective at medium and long-term follow-up.

papers.title: Risk Factors for Plantar Fasciitis in Physically Active Individuals: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 16 studies identifying three significant risk factors for PF in active populations: increased plantarflexion ROM, elevated BMI, and increased body mass.

papers.title: High-load strength training improves outcome in patients with plantar fasciitis: A randomized controlled trial with 12-month follow-up
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine and Science in Sports
papers.notes: Landmark RCT establishing high-load strength training protocol (unilateral heel raises with towel under toes) for PF; strength group had FFI 29 points lower than stretch group at 3 months.

papers.title: Management of plantar heel pain: a best practice guide informed by a systematic review, expert clinical reasoning and patient values
papers.notes: Established stepped-care treatment pathway from 51 RCTs: core treatment (taping, stretching, education), add shockwave at 4 weeks, custom orthoses at 12 weeks. Guides return-to-run decision-making.

papers.title: Biomechanical and anatomic factors associated with a history of plantar fasciitis in female runners
papers.notes: Found PF history in female runners associated with greater vertical GRF loading rates and lower medial longitudinal arch height; no difference in rearfoot motion, challenging the overpronation hypothesis.

papers.title: Plantar fasciitis injury status influences foot mechanics during running
papers.notes: Demonstrated active PF alters forefoot and midfoot mechanics during running compared to resolved cases; foot mechanics do not simply return to normal after PF resolution.

papers.title: A systematic review of running-related musculoskeletal injuries in runners
papers.journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
papers.notes: Systematic review of running-related musculoskeletal injury incidence and prevalence by anatomical location, reporting ankle-foot injuries at ~15% incidence and plantar fasciitis as a top specific diagnosis.

papers.title: The effectiveness of non-surgical interventions for common plantar digital compressive neuropathy (Morton's neuroma): a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.journal: Journal of Foot and Ankle Research
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 25 studies (7 RCTs, 18 case series) evaluating eight non-surgical interventions for Morton's neuroma, finding strongest evidence for corticosteroid injections and manipulation/mobilisation.

papers.title: Return to sport following navicular stress fracture: a systematic review and meta-analysis of three hundred and fifteen fractures
papers.notes: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 315 navicular stress fractures in athletes comparing operative (98.7% return to sport) versus conservative management outcomes and return-to-sport timelines.

papers.title: Effectiveness of foot orthoses and shock-absorbing insoles for the prevention of injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis of 18 trials finding foot orthoses effective for preventing overall injuries (RR 0.72) and stress fractures (RR 0.59) but not soft-tissue injuries; shock-absorbing insoles showed no benefit.

papers.title: Stage I and II Posterior Tibial Tendon Dysfunction: Return to Running?
papers.notes: Clinical review examining early-stage posterior tibial tendon dysfunction in runners, addressing diagnosis, conservative management with orthotics and physical therapy, and criteria for return to running versus surgical reconstruction.

papers.title: Managing Hallux Rigidus in the Elite Athlete
papers.notes: Clinical review of hallux rigidus management in elite athletes covering conservative strategies (NSAIDs, injections, shoe modifications, orthotics) and surgical options including cheilectomy for predictable return to sport.
